metadata.description: Code quality and harness-engineering plugins for Claude Code

plugins.name: harness
plugins.description: feedback-blockers, reasoning-gaps, and setup commands for harness engineering. Analyze code for feedback-loop blockers, AI reasoning gaps, and set up the harness directory structure.

plugins.name: autonomous
plugins.description: An autonomous skill that drives a single issue/ticket link or plan file all the way to an opened pull request with no human in the loop: implements, tests, simplifies the diff, runs a bounded reasoning-gaps review (critical only), reviews via core:cb-review (falling back to an independent sub-agent) to convergence, opens a PR following the target repo's conventions, and tends it through CI. Ships an autonomy contract (never stop to ask) plus a 10-rule code-style bar.

plugins.name: linting-hooks
plugins.description: PostToolUse hooks that auto-format .md files with Prettier + markdownlint and type-check .py files with pyright on every edit. Hooks self-guard when tools are missing; /linting-hooks:install sets up the dependencies per machine.

plugins.name: marketplace
plugins.description: Scaffold a Claude Code plugin marketplace with proper structure, schema validation, and CLAUDE.md conventions.

plugins.name: pyright
plugins.description: Run pyright on a Python codebase and fix its findings. Supports optional strictness override (basic/standard/strict), persisting the level to config, and progressive ratcheting.

plugins.name: codepath-visualizer
plugins.description: Map and visualize codepaths in any codebase as an interactive architecture diagram. /codepath-mapper extracts call chains from entry points into a structured JSON file; /codepath-visualizer renders the resulting graph as an interactive HTML diagram you can explore in the browser.

plugins.name: plan-keeper
plugins.description: Organize markdown plans in ~/plans/<repo>/. plan-save captures the latest plan from a conversation; plan-do lists and routes a saved plan to the right next superpowers/harness skill based on its shape (idea / spec / impl plan / task-list); plan-done archives a completed plan into ~/plans/<repo>/done/ with a completion stamp; plan-linear and plan-jira file a plan as a Linear or Jira ticket.

plugins.name: update-git-repos
plugins.description: Personal git/PR tooling. Currently ships one skill, `update-git-repos`: pull every configured git repo from origin/<branch> in one shot. Maintains a repo list at ~/.config/wild-horses/update-git-repos/repos.json, supports bootstrap auto-discovery under a root directory, manual add/remove, and prompts per repo when working trees are dirty (skip vs stash-pull-pop). Uses `git pull --ff-only` so diverged histories never auto-merge silently.

plugins.name: yes-no-questions-hook
plugins.description: Per-turn UserPromptSubmit hook that reminds Claude to pose decision questions as numbered yes/no questions, collapsing either/or choices into single yes/no items.

plugins.name: steelman
plugins.description: Argue the strongest good-faith case AGAINST the proposed changes in the current conversation or a named design/plan file. A built-in red-team voice that surfaces hidden costs, wrong assumptions, simpler alternatives, second-order effects, and the do-nothing option, so a plan gets stress-tested before it ships.

plugins.name: pr-status-hook
plugins.description: Stop hook that reports, at every turn-end, whether an open PR exists for the current branch (with its link), whether the last commits were actually pushed, and whether the working tree is dirty — all computed from real git/gh state, never from memory. Stays silent unless there is something worth reporting.

plugins.name: pr-summary-writer
plugins.description: Write architecture-first pull-request descriptions: state the one structural idea directly, give the requirements the change had to satisfy, explain the why via before/after, cover only the load-bearing decisions, and name what deliberately did not change. Replaces changelog-style descriptions (file inventories, checkbox lists, review logs) with the mental model the diff assumes. Auto-invokes whenever a PR description is about to be written or revised.

plugins.name: pr
plugins.description: Open a pull request with an architecture-first description (via pr-summary-writer), then babysit CI and review feedback three times (via cb-babysit).

plugins.name: cleanup-worktrees
plugins.description: Reclaim disk space from stale git worktrees. Scans configured roots (direct repos and parent dirs), classifies each worktree as cleanable (merged PR, upstream gone, merged into the default branch, or stale) or skipped (dirty, locked, unpushed), shows the cleanable set grouped by reason with sizes, and removes the ones you pick — re-validating before each removal, never --force, pruning the orphaned branch. Backed by a bundled cleanup_worktrees_cli.py with atomic config writes and JSON-on-stdout, plus a PreToolUse hook that auto-approves the CLI.
